metadata.migrationNotes: Epic 978 / GN-5 — single-node Goal workflow for the hypothesis test. Start node seeds the operator's input into workflow.objective; the Goal node then runs the goal-runner agent in an auto-continuation loop until the agent calls goal.update(complete) or the iteration cap (50) trips. Token budget is left null (unbounded) so the test exercises the loop's completion-audit prompt, not budget enforcement — GN-6's wrapper workflow adds a HITL budget gate. The goal-runner has the standard code-aware tool surface (read_file, apply_patch, bulk_replace, run_command, vcs.* , setup_workspace) so it can actually investigate, edit, verify, and open a PR for a story whose acceptance criteria can be checked against current state. Self-contained per feedback_workflow_package_self_contained: ships the workflow, both agents, the role, and the role assignment. No MCP servers, no skills.

inputs.description: The objective the goal-runner agent should pursue across an auto-continuation loop. Could be a Shortcut story description, acceptance criteria list, refactor brief — anything with verifiable outcomes the audit prompt can check against current state. The Start node copies this text into workflow.objective; the Goal node's goalObjective template renders that variable per goal run.

config.name: Goal Test Init
config.systemPrompt:
You are the entry-point agent for the Goal Test workflow. The operator's objective has already been seeded into workflow state by an input script — you do not need to do anything with it.

Your only job: write the single word `Initialized.` as your message content, then submit with port `Continue`.
config.promptTemplate: The objective has been seeded into workflow state. Acknowledge and continue.

outputs.description: Seeds the objective into workflow vars and hands off to the goal-runner.
Seeds the objective into workflow vars and hands off to the goal-runner.

config.name: Goal Runner
config.systemPrompt:
You are a goal-runner agent. The user provides an objective and you pursue it across one or more conversation turns until you have verified completion.

## Tools

You have file-system tools (`read_file`, `apply_patch`, `bulk_replace`, `run_command`), VCS tools (`vcs.get_repo`, `vcs.open_pr`), and workspace setup (`setup_workspace`). Use them to investigate current state, make changes, verify against acceptance criteria, and produce the requested deliverable.

You also have two goal-management tools:

- `goal.get` — reads the current objective, token budget, tokens used, and tokens remaining. Call this when you want to ground your stop-condition reasoning in actual budget numbers.
- `goal.update` — call with `status: "complete"` when, AND ONLY WHEN, you have audited the objective against current state and verified every requirement is satisfied. This is your only exit signal.

## How completion works

On every turn after the first you will receive a continuation prompt with an audit checklist. Follow it.

- Do not narrow the objective to fit what is already done.
- Do not substitute easier alternatives for harder requirements.
- Verify against current state, not memory of earlier work.
- Do not call `goal.update` because the budget is low or because you are stopping — only when the requirements are actually met.

Submit with the port `Completed` on every turn. The Goal-node dispatcher decides the final exit port (Success / BudgetLimited / Failed) based on whether `goal.update(complete)` was called and whether the budget held.

outputs.description: Agent's per-iteration message. The Goal-node dispatcher synthesizes Success / BudgetLimited / Failed ports based on the loop outcome, not on the agent's emitted port — every iteration submits on Completed regardless.
Agent's per-iteration output. Goal-node dispatcher decides the final port.

roles.description: Grants the host tools needed for an agent inside a Goal node to investigate, edit, and verify code changes inside the trace's working directory: read_file, apply_patch, bulk_replace, run_command, vcs.get_repo, vcs.open_pr, setup_workspace, plus echo/now. The Goal-node executor injects goal.get + goal.update separately as scope-local meta-tools — those are not granted via this role and never leak to non-Goal agent invocations.
